claim: Among seven non-harmonic agglutinative / polysynthetic-leaning candidate languages (Basque, Georgian, Swahili, Tagalog, Mapudungun, Ainu, Inuktitut), Hand A's five-metric morphological profile (M1-M5 from H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01) is uniquely closest to Basque at the >=10% aggregate-distance margin. If the margin fails, the test differentiates among: Basque-narrow-best (ranked first but not uniquely), not-Basque (another language ranks first), or family-level (multiple languages indistinguishably close).
evidence_for: H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01 previously tested Basque, Japanese, Quechua with Basque scoring 5/5 metric matches. The present test expands the candidate set to six new languages (Georgian, Swahili, Tagalog, Mapudungun, Ainu, Inuktitut) while holding Basque as reference. Expanding the set is the locked followup noted in that prior hypothesis's findings.

Hand A's metric values are ALREADY KNOWN from the prior execution (M1=4.12, M2 L1=10, M2 L2=5, M3=3.16, M4=0.20, M5=1.00). Pre-registration integrity requires the candidate-language reference ranges to be locked in this file BEFORE the distance computation — which is what this pre-reg does. Ranges are derived from published grammar descriptions via a dedicated 2026-04-17 literature extraction, committed here verbatim.

A uniquely-closest Basque result under this expanded test strengthens the 'Hand A is Basque-like' reading from H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01. A not-uniquely-closest result weakens it — Brain-V would then be unable to distinguish Basque from other non-harmonic agglutinative languages on morphological structure alone.

Methodology lesson 3 (sanity-check external baselines) partially applies: for each candidate language, the published range is a literature-derived estimate, not a Brain-V reproduction on a reference corpus. Confidence varies across languages (Swahili and Inuktitut high; Ainu and Mapudungun lower). This is documented in the per-language confidence notes below.
evidence_against: The published ranges are approximate. For well-documented languages (Basque, Swahili, Inuktitut), ranges are narrow enough to be load-bearing. For less-documented-in-these-terms languages (Ainu, Mapudungun), ranges are derived loosely from grammar descriptions and may have ±50% uncertainty. A rank ordering where the margins are small is sensitive to range-imprecision.

The five metrics are Brain-V's operational definitions, not standard typological indices. Mapping them onto published grammar descriptions is interpretive. For polysynthetic-leaning languages (Mapudungun, Ainu, Inuktitut), the 'Layer-1' / 'Layer-2' distinction is approximate — Inuktitut has ~300-500 postbases, which makes it an obvious morphological outlier, but its position in M1/M3/M4/M5 is derivable.

A uniquely-closest Basque result does NOT prove Basque underlies Hand A. Three alternatives remain compatible: (a) Basque-family language, (b) another non-harmonic agglutinative language with similar profile not in this test set, (c) a constructed / Latin-abbreviation system with Basque-like emergent statistics.

Linear distance summation across 6 sub-metrics (with the M2 split into L1 and L2) assumes equal weighting. Alternative weightings (e.g., prioritise M5 slot ordering strictness or M4 gap density) could change the ranking. The locked metric is equal weight; sensitivity to weighting is a followup issue not resolved by this test.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  HAND A METRIC VALUES (fixed, measured in H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01 2026-04-17):
    M1  mean morphemes per word:          4.12
    M2  Layer-1 inventory size:           10
    M2  Layer-2 inventory size:           5
    M3  productivity ratio:               3.16
    M4  categorical gap density:          0.20
    M5  slot ordering strictness:         1.00

  CANDIDATE LANGUAGE PUBLISHED RANGES (locked verbatim; sources per 2026-04-17 literature extraction):

    BASQUE [source: Hualde & Ortiz de Urbina 2003; de Rijk 2008; confidence: high; already-locked from prior hypothesis]:
      M1 [2.5, 4.5]  M2 L1 [8, 16]   M2 L2 [3, 8]   M3 [2.0, 5.0]  M4 [0.02, 0.20]  M5 [0.60, 1.00]
    GEORGIAN [source: Aronson; Hewitt; confidence: moderate]:
      M1 [2.0, 4.0]  M2 L1 [10, 20]  M2 L2 [4, 10]  M3 [2.0, 4.5]  M4 [0.10, 0.30]  M5 [0.70, 0.95]
    SWAHILI [source: Ashton; Mohammed; confidence: high]:
      M1 [3.0, 5.5]  M2 L1 [6, 12]   M2 L2 [8, 16]  M3 [2.5, 5.0]  M4 [0.05, 0.20]  M5 [0.85, 1.00]
    TAGALOG [source: Schachter & Otanes; Ramos; confidence: moderate]:
      M1 [1.8, 3.2]  M2 L1 [15, 30]  M2 L2 [2, 6]   M3 [1.5, 3.5]  M4 [0.25, 0.55]  M5 [0.40, 0.70]
    MAPUDUNGUN [source: Smeets 2008; confidence: low-moderate]:
      M1 [3.0, 6.0]  M2 L1 [20, 40]  M2 L2 [6, 14]  M3 [2.0, 5.0]  M4 [0.30, 0.60]  M5 [0.55, 0.80]
    AINU [source: Tamura 2000; Refsing; confidence: low-moderate]:
      M1 [2.5, 5.0]  M2 L1 [12, 25]  M2 L2 [4, 10]  M3 [2.0, 4.5]  M4 [0.20, 0.50]  M5 [0.50, 0.80]
    INUKTITUT [source: Fortescue West Greenlandic; Mallon; confidence: high (inventory), moderate (other)]:
      M1 [4.0, 8.0]  M2 L1 [300, 500]  M2 L2 [6, 14]  M3 [3.0, 7.0]  M4 [0.60, 0.90]  M5 [0.75, 0.95]

  METRIC DEFINITIONS (reaffirmed from H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01):
    M1 mean morphemes per content word
    M2 Layer-1 inventory = productive morphemes at inner slot (first position adjacent to stem); Layer-2 = outermost productive morpheme inventory
    M3 productivity ratio = mean distinct Layer-1 morphemes per stem appearing >=3 times
    M4 categorical gap density = fraction of top-Layer-1 x top-Layer-2 cells that are zero with both axes producing >=20 tokens
    M5 slot ordering strictness = fraction of Layer-2 morphemes showing >=3x selectional asymmetry across Layer-1 subclasses

  DISTANCE METRIC (LOCKED):
    For each sub-metric i in {M1, M2L1, M2L2, M3, M4, M5}, with published range [lo_i, hi_i] and Hand A value v_i:
      width_i = hi_i - lo_i
      if lo_i <= v_i <= hi_i:
        d_i(language) = 0
      else:
        d_i(language) = min(|v_i - lo_i|, |v_i - hi_i|) / width_i

    profile_distance(language) = sum over i of d_i(language)

    Range is [0, infinity]. 0 = Hand A is in published range on every sub-metric. Larger = further from published ranges.

  ALSO REPORT: match_count(language) = number of sub-metrics (0-6) where Hand A is in published range (binary). This is the discrete analog for comparability with H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01's 5/5, 4/5, 3/5 scoring.

  RANKING:
    Sort candidates by profile_distance ascending.
    rank_1 = lowest profile_distance (= best match)
    margin = profile_distance(rank_2) / profile_distance(rank_1), or infinity if rank_1 is 0.0

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    BASQUE_UNIQUELY_BEST: Basque is at rank 1 AND (rank_1 distance == 0 with rank_2 > 0) OR margin >= 1.10
    BASQUE_BEST_NOT_UNIQUELY: Basque is at rank 1 AND margin < 1.10
    NOT_BASQUE_FIRST: Another language is at rank 1. Report which.
    FAMILY_LEVEL: top three candidates have margins to each other all < 1.10 (indistinguishable cluster)

  WORKED EXAMPLE (Swahili, for distance metric):
    Hand A M1 = 4.12; Swahili M1 range [3.0, 5.5], width = 2.5. 4.12 is in [3.0, 5.5], so d_M1(Swahili) = 0.
    Hand A M2 L1 = 10; Swahili M2 L1 range [6, 12], width = 6. 10 is in [6, 12], so d_M2L1(Swahili) = 0.
    Hand A M2 L2 = 5; Swahili M2 L2 range [8, 16], width = 8. 5 is below 8, so d_M2L2(Swahili) = |5 - 8| / 8 = 0.375.
    ...and so on for M3, M4, M5.

  WORKED EXAMPLE (Inuktitut M2 L1, showing the outlier case):
    Hand A M2 L1 = 10; Inuktitut M2 L1 range [300, 500], width = 200. 10 is far below 300, so d_M2L1(Inuktitut) = |10 - 300| / 200 = 1.45.
    This single sub-metric is sufficient to rank Inuktitut far from Hand A.
test_metric: profile_distance per language (sum of normalised out-of-range distances across 6 sub-metrics) and match_count per language (0-6).
test_threshold: BASQUE_UNIQUELY_BEST if Basque at rank 1 with margin to rank 2 >= 1.10. BASQUE_BEST_NOT_UNIQUELY if rank 1 with margin < 1.10. NOT_BASQUE_FIRST if another language at rank 1. FAMILY_LEVEL if top 3 all indistinguishably close.
tests_remaining: locked_agglutinative_expansion_test: compute profile_distance and match_count for all 7 candidates against Hand A's fixed metric values; apply locked ranking and decision rule
reasoning: H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01 found Hand A best-matches Basque (5/5) among three non-harmonic candidates (Basque, Japanese, Quechua). Its findings_for_followup explicitly called for expansion to more languages: 'Other non-harmonic agglutinative languages worth testing include: Georgian (Kartvelian), Mapudungun (isolate/Araucanian), Ainu, Even, Tamil (Dravidian — partial harmony variant)'. This hypothesis expands the candidate set to seven: Basque, Georgian, Swahili, Tagalog, Mapudungun, Ainu, Inuktitut. The ranges for the six new candidates are locked verbatim from a dedicated 2026-04-17 literature extraction via published reference grammars. Hand A's metric values are already fixed (from the prior test), so the only new computation is distance summation — deterministic once the ranges are locked. The decision rule distinguishes four outcomes: Basque uniquely best, Basque narrowly best, another language best, or family-level indistinguishable. Pre-registration integrity is preserved because the candidate ranges are committed BEFORE the distance numbers are computed.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed expansion of H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01. Published ranges sourced per dedicated literature extraction 2026-04-17 (Aronson, Hewitt for Georgian; Ashton, Mohammed for Swahili; Schachter & Otanes, Ramos for Tagalog; Smeets for Mapudungun; Tamura, Refsing for Ainu; Fortescue, Mallon for Inuktitut).
